text.title: RAG Generation and Citation Rules
text.summary: Rules for the RAG generation prompt structure, faithfulness constraint enforcement, citation format, and post-generation faithfulness verification.

rule.statement: Instruct the model to answer ONLY from the provided context. Include an explicit fallback instruction: "If the context does not contain the answer, say so."
rule.rationale: Without this constraint, the model falls back to its training knowledge and produces answers that appear grounded but are not. The fallback instruction prevents confident hallucination.

rule.statement: Always request citations in a consistent format: [Source: filename, page N]. Parse and validate them against retrieved chunk metadata post-generation.
rule.rationale: Citations without validation are unverified assertions. The model may cite a plausible-sounding source that was not actually retrieved. Validation against chunk metadata catches hallucinated citations.

rule.statement: Run a faithfulness evaluator on production responses. When faithfulness score is below 0.8, queue for human review rather than surfacing to users.
rule.rationale: Low faithfulness means the generated answer contains claims not supported by the retrieved context. These are hallucinations that can damage user trust and cause compliance failures.

example.note: Citation format is specified explicitly in the prompt; the fallback instruction prevents hallucination when context is insufficient.

text.reference: System + context prompt template enforcing "answer only from context"
